behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from recommendation results regardless of relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between inventory availability filtering and ranking: 'excluded ... regardless of their relevance score' requires both modules wired together. Unit was rejected because single-function scoring/filter logic would miss ordering-of-operations bugs across modules. System was rejected because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; in-process catalog + inventory fixtures reproduce the failure. Agentic/Workflow were rejected because no AI/LLM dependency is described.
behaviors.test_description: Wire recommendation pipeline with real filter + rank components and fixture data containing high-score out-of-stock items; assert none appear in final ranked page.

behaviors.description: User preference filter excludes categories explicitly blocked in user settings.
behaviors.justification: Bug appears at settings-to-filter boundary (user profile/settings module feeding catalog filter). Unit was rejected because isolated predicate tests do not catch mapping/serialization handoff issues. System rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text. Agentic/Workflow rejected: deterministic preference rules, no model-output quality dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run recommender with persisted user settings including blocked categories and mixed catalog; assert blocked-category items are absent end-to-end.

behaviors.description: Algorithm ranks products matching recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: This checks feature extraction + scoring + ranking interaction, not just one pure scorer function. Unit rejected because ranking failures often come from incorrect feature wiring from browsing history into scorer. Agentic rejected by behavior text: it describes an internal recommendation algorithm, not a real AI/LLM call; correctness can be validated with deterministic fixtures. System rejected: no real external infra required.
behaviors.test_description: Construct user history and candidate products where only subset has browsing-signal affinity; run full ranking stack and assert signal-matching items outrank no-signal controls.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns correct page with stable ordering when underlying data is unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Requires interaction between ranking order and pagination slicing; failures are typically at module boundary (sort key/tie-breaker propagation). Unit rejected because testing paginator alone misses unstable upstream ordering. System rejected because behavior does not require real network/OS events; deterministic in-process repository is sufficient. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model decision dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Generate fixed recommendation set with ties, request page 2 twice without data changes, and assert identical ordered IDs plus correct offset/limit behavior.

behaviors.description: Algorithm surfaces at least one product from an unbrowsed category (serendipity).
behaviors.justification: Serendipity here is a deterministic product-mix constraint within the recommendation pipeline (candidate generation + diversification + ranking). Unit rejected because single-function tests can miss where diversification is dropped after ranking/pagination integration. Agentic rejected: behavior text does not require real AI/LLM judgment quality, only rule/constraint satisfaction. System rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess dependency.
behaviors.test_description: With user history concentrated in category A and catalog across A/B/C, run full pipeline and assert at least one top-N result is from an unbrowsed category.

behaviors.description: New user with no history gets popularity-based recommendations, not empty results.
behaviors.justification: Cold-start fallback is an orchestration boundary: history lookup -> strategy selection -> popularity ranker. Unit rejected because isolated fallback function tests can miss misclassification of 'new user' state in pipeline. System rejected: no real OS/network/subprocess required. Agentic/Workflow rejected: popularity fallback is deterministic business logic per behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Create user with empty purchase/browse history; run recommender with popularity source and assert non-empty output ordered by popularity baseline.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records correct product ID, list position, and timestamp for each interaction.
behaviors.justification: Failure occurs at UI/event payload -> tracking service -> persistence schema boundary. Unit rejected because serializer-only tests miss field drift across modules. System considered but rejected: behavior text does not require real external analytics infra; local datastore + injectable clock catches correctness failures. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI/LLM output dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Emit recommendation click events through tracking pipeline with controlled clock; assert persisted records contain exact product_id, rank position, and timestamp per click.

behaviors.description: Recommendation generation stays under 200ms for a 100,000-product catalog.
behaviors.justification: This is a real performance SLO dependent on runtime/process behavior and dataset scale; in-process mocked timing can hide true latency. Integration rejected because failure boundary includes actual execution characteristics (CPU/memory/runtime scheduling) under realistic load. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no LLM/model-output quality requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run the production recommendation service/process with a 100k-item realistic dataset and measure end-to-end request latency (p95/p99 as defined) to verify <200ms threshold.

behaviors.description: After user purchases a recommended product, subsequent recommendations deprioritize it.
behaviors.justification: Requires state handoff across modules: recommendation exposure, purchase event ingestion, profile/update store, then next ranking pass. Unit rejected because isolated scorer tests miss event-to-state propagation bugs. System rejected: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess; local modules + DB fixture can reproduce. Agentic/Workflow rejected: deterministic ranking policy change, no model-output dependency specified.
behaviors.test_description: Generate recommendations, record a purchase for one recommended product, request recommendations again, and assert purchased product rank is lower (or excluded) per policy.

behaviors.description: Recommendations are deterministic for unchanged user state across repeated requests.
behaviors.justification: Determinism depends on stable ordering/tie-breakers across retrieval + ranking + pagination modules. Unit rejected because pure sorter tests miss nondeterminism introduced by upstream module interactions. System considered but rejected: behavior text is scoped to unchanged state, not real distributed/network nondeterminism; in-process wired modules can catch real logic instability. Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI/LLM stochastic output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed user/profile/catalog snapshots and fixed seeds/config, invoke recommender twice and assert identical ranked product ID sequence.
